serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of grilled Japanese tofu steak weighing approximately 300 g, typically served with steamed rice or vegetables and a savoury soy-based glaze.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Tofu, firm, prepared with calcium sulfate and magnesium chloride (nigari) (SR Legacy #16127) and Soy sauce (SR Legacy #16124), composite estimate for grilled tofu steak dish
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA entry exists for 'Grilled Japanese Tofu Steak' as a composite dish. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entries for firm tofu (#16127) and soy sauce (#16124), weighted to reflect a typical grilled tofu steak preparation (approximately 85% firm tofu, 10% soy-mirin glaze, 5% sesame oil and aromatics). The input record's per-100g values (energy 126.67 kcal, protein 8.33 g, fat 6.67 g, carbohydrate 8.33 g) are consistent with this composite and are retained with medium confidence. Sodium at 283.3 mg/100g reflects soy sauce contribution and is plausible. All values cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central composite logic.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 'Grilled Japanese Tofu Steak' as a composite dish. GI of 15 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for tofu / soybean curd (GI ≈ 15, low category), which constitutes approximately 85% of the dish by weight. The soy-mirin glaze contributes a small carbohydrate load but does not materially raise the overall GI given the dominant protein and fat matrix. Glycemic load per 300 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (15 × 24.99) / 100 ≈ 3.75, rounded to 4.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content of 283 mg per 100 g, primarily from soy sauce glaze; a 300 g serving delivers approximately 850 mg sodium, representing 37% of the 2,300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced-sodium soy sauce or ask for the sauce on the side to limit sodium intake; pairing with potassium-rich vegetables such as broccoli or spinach can partially offset the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI ≈ 15) driven by high plant protein (8.33 g/100 g) and fat content from firm tofu, with moderate carbohydrate (8.33 g/100 g) and low sugar (2.67 g/100 g).
type2_diabetes.guidance: Grilled Japanese tofu steak is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low GI and high plant protein content; if served with rice, choose a small portion of brown rice and monitor total carbohydrate intake to stay within the recommended 45–60 g per meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (1.0 g/100 g), zero trans fat, zero cholesterol, and high plant protein with soy isoflavones, which have documented LDL-lowering effects.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Grilled Japanese tofu steak is a heart-friendly option due to its low saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and soy isoflavone content; the primary cardiovascular caution is the moderate sodium from soy sauce, which should be minimised by requesting a reduced-sodium preparation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled Japanese Tofu Steak (焼き豆腐ステーキ, Yaki Tōfu Sutēki; Chinese: 日式烤豆腐排; Malay: Stik Tauhu Panggang Jepun) is a Japanese-inspired dish consisting of thick slices of firm tofu that are grilled or pan-seared until golden and slightly charred on the exterior, then glazed with a savoury sauce typically made from soy sauce, mirin, sake, ginger, and garlic. The dish is commonly garnished with spring onions, sesame seeds, and occasionally served alongside steamed rice or lightly blanched vegetables such as broccoli or bok choy. The texture contrasts a lightly crisp outer surface with a soft, silken interior. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 127 kcal, 8.3 g plant protein, 6.7 g fat (predominantly polyunsaturated from tofu and sesame), 8.3 g carbohydrate, and 283 mg sodium. The glycemic index is low (approximately 15), making it suitable for blood glucose management. Soy isoflavones in tofu contribute anti-inflammatory and LDL-lowering properties relevant to cardiovascular health. The primary clinical caution is the moderate sodium content from the soy-based glaze, which should be managed in hypertensive patients by requesting reduced-sodium soy sauce. This dish is fully vegetarian and vegan, and is a popular healthy option in Japanese restaurants and food delivery menus in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Japanese tofu steak?
Is tofu steak good for diabetics?
Can I eat tofu steak if I have high blood pressure?
What is yaki tofu steak made of?
Is tofu steak good for heart health?

confidence_reason: No single approved source entry exists for 'Grilled Japanese Tofu Steak' as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for firm tofu and soy sauce, weighted by typical recipe proportions. The input values are internally consistent and plausible. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for tofu. Medium confidence is appropriate for a composite dish estimate from approved sources.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (firm tofu #16127 and soy sauce #16124) as no single approved-source entry exists for this composite dish. Input per-100g values retained as they are consistent with the USDA composite estimate. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid fractionation data for this specific grilled tofu steak composite preparation are not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero for tofu-based plant protein dish per USDA FoodData Central; naturally zero in this plant-based preparation. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a fully plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients. 4. The input record listed duplicate portions (two identical '1 plate / 300 g' entries); only one serving entry is retained. 5. Ethnic tags 'Sushi', 'Tempura', and 'Grilled Fish' from the input are not applicable to this dish and have been removed. 6. The input health_suitability entries incorrectly marked all three conditions as 'suitable: false' while the reasoning supported suitability with caution; health_context has been corrected to reflect accurate clinical assessment. 7. GI of 15 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database tofu entry; no direct GI measurement for grilled Japanese tofu steak as a composite dish was found in approved sources. 8. This dish is served in Singapore under a Japanese cuisine category; multilingual local names have been added for Chinese (日式烤豆腐排), Malay (Stik Tauhu Panggang Jepun), Tamil (ஜப்பானிய வறுத்த டோஃபு ஸ்டீக்), and Hindi (जापानी ग्रिल्ड टोफू स्टेक) to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance for the Singapore serving context, in addition to the mandatory Japanese name. 9. Aliases self-check: all strings in aliases_by_language (english, chinese, malay, tamil, hindi, japanese, romanized) verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. Q8 self-check passed.
